pairs.name: Chapter 1
leftData: 当死亡降临给一个对此毫无概念的生命时，这不仅仅是杀戮，这是一种卑鄙的偷袭。
这种难受来自于一种契约的单方面撕毁：它以为这个世界是用来探索的，而人类知道这个世界是用来吃它的。
这种利用对方的“天真”来实施的暴力，在审美上是极其丑陋的。
rightData: When death befalls a life that has no concept of it, it is not merely killing; it is a cowardly ambush.
This distress stems from the unilateral breaking of a contract: the creature believed the world was for exploration, while humans knew the world was for consuming it.
This violence, carried out by exploiting the other party's "innocence," is aesthetically repulsive.
